description: Extend `ValidateQueryMiddleware` unit coverage to lock the existing query-field casing contract. Acceptance: a schema-defined mixed-case query-field name such as `SchoolId` is accepted during Core query validation; the resulting `QueryElement.QueryFieldName` preserves the client-supplied casing instead of being canonicalized; invalid query-field names still fail with the existing 400 behavior; and no production code changes are required unless a test exposes an existing Core mismatch.
steps-to-verify: Add or update tests in `./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/Middleware/ValidateQueryMiddlewareTests.cs` covering accepted mixed-case field names, preserved `QueryElement.QueryFieldName`, and unchanged invalid-field rejection.

description: Make compiled relational query capability metadata case-insensitive by query-field name. Acceptance: `RelationalQueryCapabilityCompiler` creates both `SupportedFieldsByQueryField` and `UnsupportedFieldsByQueryField` with `StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase`; canonical query-field names remain the stored `QueryFieldName` values on the compiled records; supported and unsupported query-field classification behavior is otherwise unchanged; and mixed-case dictionary lookups succeed for compiled capability metadata.
steps-to-verify: Update compiler coverage in `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/MappingSetCompilerTests.cs` to prove supported and unsupported query-field metadata can be retrieved case-insensitively without changing the stored canonical names.

description: Update `RelationalQueryRequestPreprocessor` to honor case-insensitive query-field names and remove repeated fragile string-key lookups. Acceptance: each `QueryElement` resolves its `SupportedRelationalQueryField` once using the case-insensitive capability dictionary; descriptor pre-resolution carries the resolved supported field instead of looking it up again by string key later; failure messages continue using the original client-supplied `QueryElement.QueryFieldName`; and preprocessing succeeds for mixed-case root-column, `id`, reference-identity, and descriptor query-field names while preserving current invalid-id and unresolved-descriptor empty-page behavior.
steps-to-verify: Add or update tests in `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/RelationalQueryRequestPreprocessorTests.cs` covering mixed-case root-column, mixed-case `ID`, mixed-case reference-identity, and mixed-case descriptor query-field names.

description: Add deterministic plan-compilation validation for query-field names that collide case-insensitively. Acceptance: if `queryFieldMapping` contains two entries whose names differ only by casing, compilation fails with an actionable message before any case-insensitive dictionary silently overwrites an entry; non-colliding resources continue compiling unchanged; and the guard executes in a stable compilation path such as `QueryFieldMappingsExtractor` or `RelationalQueryCapabilityCompiler`.
steps-to-verify: Add compiler-focused tests in `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit` proving case-insensitive query-field collisions fail deterministically and ordinary query-field mappings still compile.

description: Add repository-level regression coverage proving mixed-case relational query-field names no longer collapse into `UnknownFailure`. Acceptance: a mixed-case supported query-field name reaches normal relational query planning or the expected preprocessing short-circuit path instead of returning `UnknownFailure`; the test demonstrates that casing alone cannot turn a Core-validated relational GET-many request into HTTP 500; and the regression coverage runs through `RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.QueryDocuments` rather than only through lower-level preprocessing helpers.
steps-to-verify: Add or update tests in `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/RelationalDocumentStoreRepositoryTests.cs` proving mixed-case supported query fields no longer return `UnknownFailure` and still follow the expected repository behavior.

description: Introduce plan-scoped bulk-reconstitution metadata for relational reads. Acceptance: add `ScopeKey`, `CompiledReconstitutionPlan`, and `TableReconstitutionPlan` (or equivalent) plus a builder/cache keyed by `ResourceReadPlan`; the builder resolves and stores table-local root-scope locator, immediate-parent locator, physical-row-identity, and ordinal ordinals from compiled read-plan metadata; groups `ReferenceIdentityProjectionBinding`s by table once; flattens `DescriptorProjectionPlansInOrder[*].SourcesInOrder` into table-local descriptor bindings using compiled descriptor ordinals instead of `Model.DescriptorEdgeSources`; builds the property-order tree once; `ScopeKey` uses structural equality over canonicalized parts; and the compiled shape preserves list-based locator/identity metadata even though the first-pass implementation still validates single-column use.
steps-to-verify: Add new unit coverage in `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/CompiledReconstitutionPlanTests.cs` proving reference bindings, descriptor bindings, ordinals, `ScopeKey` equality, and cached property ordering are derived once from `ResourceReadPlan`.

description: Derive and validate page-level parent/child table topology once during compiled reconstitution-plan construction. Acceptance: every non-root table gets exactly one `ImmediateParentTable`; `ImmediateChildrenInDependencyOrder` is emitted deterministically; parent tables always appear earlier in dependency order; allowed attachment pairs are enforced for root collections, nested collections, root extension scopes, collection extension scopes, and extension child collections; and `_ext` child collections such as `$.addresses[*]._ext.sample.services[*]` attach through the aligned extension scope table instead of directly to the base collection scope.
steps-to-verify: Add unit coverage in `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/PageReconstitutionTopologyTests.cs` for root collections, nested collections, root extensions, collection extension scopes, extension child collections, and invalid or ambiguous parent derivation.

description: Add page-scoped row-graph construction for hydrated query pages. Acceptance: introduce `PageReconstitutionContext`, `DocumentPageNode`, and `RowNode` (or equivalent); build one descriptor URI lookup per `HydratedPage`; create root nodes keyed by `DocumentId`; canonicalize provider-shaped numeric locator and identity values to `long` before constructing first-pass `ScopeKey([longValue])` instances; build table-qualified physical-identity lookups; attach every non-root row to its resolved immediate parent exactly once; preserve `HydratedPage.DocumentMetadata` order in `DocumentsInOrder`; and fail fast on duplicate root rows, duplicate physical row identities, orphaned child rows, metadata rows without roots, or extra roots not present in metadata.
steps-to-verify: Add unit coverage in `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/PageReconstitutionContextTests.cs` proving numeric canonicalization, single-build descriptor lookup, row attachment, ordered `DocumentsInOrder`, and the expected fail-fast defects.

description: Move child-row ordering work out of per-document reconstitution and into page construction. Acceptance: sibling lists are validated for monotonic hydration order or sorted exactly once per `(parent, child table)` using the compiled `Ordinal` metadata; `DocumentReconstituter` no longer depends on per-document `ChildRowIndex.Create(...)` regrouping and sorting to restore array order; and nested collections plus extension collections keep deterministic JSON order across all documents in the page.
steps-to-verify: Add unit coverage in `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/PageReconstitutionContextTests.cs` or `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/DocumentReconstituterTests.cs` proving array order is preserved by one page-build-time step and not by repeated per-document indexing.

description: Refactor `DocumentReconstituter` to emit documents from the page row graph instead of rediscovering page structure per document. Acceptance: add a page-first entry point such as `ReconstituteDocument(DocumentPageNode, PageReconstitutionContext)`; root scalars, references, descriptors, child collections, and `_ext` scopes are emitted from attached `RowNode` children in compiled dependency order; descriptor strings come from the page lookup; property reordering uses the cached property-order tree instead of rebuilding it per document; and representative nested-plus-`_ext` fixtures remain semantically equivalent to the current JSON output.
steps-to-verify: Add or update coverage in `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/DocumentReconstituterTests.cs` proving page-based reconstitution matches existing JSON semantics for root scalars, nested collections, references, descriptors, and `_ext`.

description: Extend the relational read materializer with a page-first API without breaking current single-document callers. Acceptance: add `RelationalReadPageMaterializationRequest`, `MaterializedDocument`, and `IRelationalReadMaterializer.MaterializePage(...)` (or equivalent); `RelationalReadMaterializer` builds or retrieves the compiled reconstitution plan once per `ResourceReadPlan`, builds one `PageReconstitutionContext` per hydrated page, returns documents in `HydratedPage.DocumentMetadata` order, injects `id`/`_etag`/`_lastModifiedDate` only for `ExternalResponse`, and keeps the existing single-document `Materialize(...)` behavior valid for GET-by-id and write-side callers.
steps-to-verify: Add or update tests in `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/RelationalReadMaterializerTests.cs` proving `MaterializePage(...)` emits ordered results, builds descriptor lookup once per page, preserves stored-document vs external-response metadata behavior, and keeps single-document materialization working.

description: Cut the query path over to page-based materialization. Acceptance: `RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.BuildQuerySuccess()` calls `_readMaterializer.MaterializePage(...)` exactly once per hydrated page instead of looping `DocumentMetadata` with single-document `Materialize(...)`; readable-profile projection still executes per returned item; `_etag` is refreshed after profile projection; query item order still matches hydrated page order; and `totalCount=true` behavior remains unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Add or update repository-focused tests in `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/RelationalDocumentStoreRepositoryTests.cs` proving query execution makes one page materialization call, preserves returned ids/order, still applies readable-profile projection, and refreshes `_etag` after projection.

description: Add regression coverage that locks the new page-first query reconstitution path end to end. Acceptance: update recorder-based unit tests to assert page materialization call count and returned document ids instead of per-document materializer calls; keep existing relational query execution tests passing; and add at least one multi-document query-page scenario on both PostgreSQL and SQL Server with child rows plus `_ext` or descriptor data so the provider-sensitive query path exercises page-scoped reconstitution rather than a hidden per-document fallback.
steps-to-verify: Add or update provider integration coverage in `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/PostgresqlRelationalQueryExecutionTests.cs` and `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/MssqlRelationalQueryExecutionTests.cs`, plus any supporting repository/unit recorder tests needed for call-count assertions.

description: Add a narrow `ReferenceIdentityQueryTargetResolver` in `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans` to inventory root-table reference identity candidates for query compilation. Acceptance: the helper scans only `RelationalResourceModel.DocumentReferenceBindings` whose `Table` equals the resource root table; builds candidate records that retain `IdentityJsonPath`, `ReferenceJsonPath`, bound `Column`, owning `TargetResource`, owning `ReferenceObjectPath`, and owning `FkColumn`; calls `ReferenceIdentityProjectionLogicalFieldResolver.ResolveOrThrow(...)` for each root binding and attaches each raw candidate to that logical field's representative binding column; validates candidate member columns exist on the root table and have matching `DbColumnModel.SourceJsonPath`; never parses physical column names; and exposes deterministic no-match, ambiguous, and fail-fast outcomes for `RelationalQueryCapabilityCompiler` to consume.
steps-to-verify: Add `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/ReferenceIdentityQueryTargetResolverTests.cs` covering candidate inventory, root-table filtering, same-site duplicate `ReferenceJsonPath` grouping, representative binding-column selection, and missing or mismatched member-column failures.

description: Teach the reference query resolver to convert a matched candidate into the correct `RelationalQueryFieldTarget` while preserving binding-vs-storage semantics. Acceptance: scalar reference identity candidates return `RootColumn(representativeBindingColumn)` only when the representative root column is `ColumnKind.Scalar` with scalar type metadata; descriptor-valued candidates return `DescriptorIdColumn(representativeBindingColumn, descriptorResource)` by resolving root `DescriptorEdgeSource` metadata by `(Table, FkColumn)`; descriptor duplicates collapse only when every candidate resolves to the same descriptor resource; invalid column kind, missing scalar metadata, missing descriptor metadata, or divergent descriptor resources fail fast as model/plan compilation errors instead of becoming unsupported fields; and `UnifiedAlias` binding columns are returned as aliases so canonical rewrite and presence gates remain owned by `PageDocumentIdSqlCompiler`.
steps-to-verify: Extend `ReferenceIdentityQueryTargetResolverTests.cs` with scalar target, descriptor target, `UnifiedAlias` target, descriptor-resource mismatch, missing descriptor metadata, and invalid representative-column cases.

description: Update `RelationalQueryCapabilityCompiler` exact root-path resolution to collapse supported same-site duplicate targets before returning `AmbiguousRootTarget`. Acceptance: unique exact descriptor and scalar path matches continue compiling unchanged; multiple exact root scalar matches collapse only when all candidates belong to one same-site logical reference field group and resolve to the group's representative API-bound binding column; multiple exact descriptor matches collapse only when they are one same-site logical field group and resolve to the same descriptor resource; duplicates across different reference sites or non-converging storage columns still classify as `AmbiguousRootTarget`; and exact ambiguity collapse does not use virtual alias heuristics.
steps-to-verify: Add or update compiler tests in `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/MappingSetCompilerTests.cs` or a dedicated `RelationalQueryCapabilityCompilerTests.cs` for same-site scalar collapse, same-site descriptor collapse, unique exact matches, and cross-site ambiguous duplicates.

description: Implement the virtual reference identity alias matcher used only after a single-path query field has passed the no-array and root-table checks and would otherwise be `UnmappedPath`. Acceptance: the matcher maps CourseTranscript-style `studentUniqueId` with path `$.studentReference.studentAcademicRecordUniqueId` to `RootColumn(StudentAcademicRecord_StudentUniqueId)` while preserving the public query path; maps StudentAssessmentRegistration-style `studentUniqueId` and `scheduledStudentUniqueId` aliases to their generated local binding columns; maps one general student-program association alias such as `$.studentReference.generalStudentProgramAssociationUniqueId` to the local student binding column when the guards leave exactly one candidate; maps one non-student alias such as `BusRoute.staffUniqueId` to the local staff binding column; matches only through query-field leaf/suffix guards, query parent-reference leaf/suffix guards, and `<lowerCamel(TargetResource.ResourceName)>UniqueId` preference; and does not add edit-distance, token-search, reference-resource join, or column-name fallback behavior.
steps-to-verify: Add focused unit cases in `RelationalQueryCapabilityCompilerTests.cs` or `MappingSetCompilerTests.cs` for CourseTranscript, StudentAssessmentRegistration, scheduled StudentAssessmentRegistration, StudentCTEProgramAssociation, and BusRoute-style virtual aliases.

description: Complete deterministic unsupported/ambiguous classifications for virtual reference alias resolution. Acceptance: `ArrayCrossing` and `NonRootTable` classifications are not overridden by virtual alias fallback; a query path leaf that does not match `<lowerCamel(TargetResource.ResourceName)>UniqueId` remains unsupported unless the field-name and parent-reference guards leave exactly one `*UniqueId` candidate; multiple remaining candidates that do not converge to one same-site logical field classify as `AmbiguousRootTarget`; same-site duplicates that converge through key unification collapse to the representative binding column; virtual alias matching never picks a target by physical column-name convention; and existing `RelationalQueryFieldFailureKind` values remain sufficient for manifest diagnostics.
steps-to-verify: Add unit coverage for target-resource-prefix misses, generic `*UniqueId` fallback success, generic fallback ambiguity, same-site duplicate collapse, cross-site ambiguity, array-crossing preservation, and non-root preservation.

description: Wire the reference-aware resolver into `RelationalQueryCapabilityCompiler.CompileQueryField` with the smallest flow change that matches `PATH-RESOLUTION-FIX.md`. Acceptance: multi-path mappings and the `$.id` special case behave exactly as today; exact root descriptor resolution runs before exact root scalar resolution; exact ambiguous descriptor and scalar matches call the same-site collapse path before returning `AmbiguousRootTarget`; `ArrayCrossing` and `NonRootTable` are classified before virtual fallback; virtual fallback is tried only for otherwise unmapped root-reference aliases; `SupportedRelationalQueryField.Path` remains the original ApiSchema query path even when the predicate target is a different local binding column; supported and unsupported dictionaries remain case-insensitive by query field name; and `PageDocumentIdSqlCompiler` is unchanged unless a planner test exposes a missing alias-rewrite behavior.
steps-to-verify: Add a planner/preprocessor contract test proving a CourseTranscript-style supported field keeps `$.studentReference.studentAcademicRecordUniqueId` in `SupportedRelationalQueryField.Path` and in `QueryElement.DocumentPaths[0]` while targeting `RootColumn(StudentAcademicRecord_StudentUniqueId)`.

description: Update mapping-set manifest and authoritative-schema regression coverage for reference identity query aliases. Acceptance: regenerated query capability diagnostics show deterministic reference identity aliases such as `CourseTranscript.studentUniqueId` in `supported_fields_in_query_field_order` with their original ApiSchema query paths and root-column targets; those aliases no longer appear in `unsupported_fields_in_query_field_order` as `unmapped_path`; resources omitted solely because of deterministic reference identity aliases become `RelationalQuerySupport.Supported`; genuinely unsupported multi-path, array-crossing, non-root, and ambiguous cases remain unsupported; and authoritative `sample` and `ds-5.2` schema compilation is covered without changing ApiSchema fixture query paths.
steps-to-verify: Add or update manifest/golden coverage in `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/MappingSetManifestJsonEmitterTests.cs`, `MappingSetManifestGoldenFixtureTests.cs`, or a new authoritative query-capability regression fixture.
When golden files are intentionally updated, run the existing golden update workflow with `UPDATE_GOLDENS=1` for the affected mapping-set manifest tests.

description: Add real PostgreSQL and SQL Server relational GET-many regression coverage for reference identity query aliases. Acceptance: each provider test seeds or writes a CourseTranscript-like resource graph with a local reference identity binding column; executes a relational query equivalent to `GET /courseTranscripts?studentUniqueId=...` with empty/no-op authorization or `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired`; returns success/HTTP 200 with the expected document rather than `QueryUnsupported`/501; verifies the query path uses one page keyset and one page materialization, not get-by-id lookup; and asserts the generated page-selection SQL predicates on the local root-table binding column or its canonical alias rewrite without joining to the referenced resource table.
steps-to-verify: Add or update provider coverage in `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/PostgresqlRelationalQueryExecutionTests.cs` and `./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/MssqlRelationalQueryExecutionTests.cs`.
